text:
There once was a lonely old owl who lived deep in an ancient forest.

Each morning he would glide lazily across the leafy canopy, scanning for sparrows. One particular day, his sharp eyes caught a flash of blue—a young sparrow!

That night, our wise owl crafted a basket out of twigs and feathers. By dawn, little Sparrow hops inside.

"Stay safe," whispers the owl.

"Thanks! I won't forget you!" chirps Sparrow.

Together, they feast on insects. Weeks turn into seasons. Sparrow turns feather-white.

The old owl grows melancholic. He hasn't seen Sparrow since.

One autumn morning, a gust sweeps through. Sparrow swoops out!

"Hooo! Hello!"

The old owl leaps out. They fly around together. Sparrow sings sweet tunes. They glide back into their nests.

From that day forward, young Sparrows sing Sparrow songs.

And so our wise, though lonely, owl enjoyed many happy mornings
